pillar.metaDescription: Final expense insurance explained: costs, coverage, and how to qualify even with health issues. Compare burial plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
pillar.heroSubhead: Small whole life policies built to cover funeral, burial, and end-of-life costs — compare options in one call.
pillar.quickAnswer: Final expense insurance is a small whole life policy, typically $5,000 to $40,000, designed to cover funeral, burial, and final medical costs. Most plans require no medical exam, build cash value, and never expire if premiums are paid. The 2023 average U.S. funeral with burial cost about $8,300 (NFDA).
pillar.intro: Final expense insurance helps seniors leave loved ones with cash, not bills, when they pass. Here is how it works, what it costs, and how to qualify.

sections.h2: What is final expense insurance and how does it work?
sections.body: Final expense insurance is a permanent whole life policy with a smaller death benefit than traditional life insurance, usually $5,000 to $40,000. It is built to cover the bills that arrive at the end of life: funeral and burial or cremation, outstanding medical bills, and small debts. Premiums stay level for life, the policy never expires as long as you pay, and it builds modest cash value. Coverage pays a tax-free lump sum to your named beneficiary, who can use it however they choose. Because benefit amounts are modest and underwriting is simple, these policies are easier to qualify for than most life insurance.

sections.h2: How much does final expense insurance cost?
sections.body: Premiums depend on your age, gender, health, tobacco use, and the death benefit you choose. As a general guide, a healthy non-smoker in their 60s often pays roughly $30 to $70 a month for $10,000 in coverage, while rates rise with age and benefit size. Guaranteed issue plans cost more because they skip health questions. Because rates vary widely by carrier and state, the only way to know your real number is a personalized quote. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will compare carriers for you.

sections.h2: Who qualifies, and what if I have health problems?
sections.body: Most seniors qualify. Final expense plans come in two main types. Simplified issue asks a short list of health questions, offers lower rates, and usually pays the full benefit from day one. Guaranteed issue asks no health questions and accepts nearly everyone, but typically uses a two- or three-year graded period during which only premiums plus interest are refunded for non-accidental death. If you have diabetes, heart disease, COPD, or other conditions, you can still get covered — the right choice depends on your health history and budget, which a licensed agent can match for you.

sections.h2: Final expense vs. burial insurance vs. pre-need plans
sections.body: "Final expense," "burial," and "funeral" insurance usually describe the same product: a small whole life policy paid in cash to a beneficiary. The terms are marketing labels, not different legal categories. Pre-need insurance is different — it is bought directly through a funeral home and locks in specific services with that provider, so the money is tied to one funeral home. Final expense gives your family flexibility because the cash can go toward any funeral home, burial, cremation, or remaining bills. For most seniors, that flexibility makes final expense the more practical choice.

sections.h2: How to compare and buy a final expense policy
sections.body: Start by deciding how much coverage your family would need — add up expected funeral or cremation costs, plus any debts you want cleared. The 2023 median U.S. funeral with burial ran about $8,300 (National Funeral Directors Association), and cremation services average less. Next, compare quotes from several A-rated carriers, since prices for identical coverage can differ by hundreds of dollars a year. Check whether coverage is immediate or graded, and confirm the carrier's financial strength rating. The fastest path is one call: dial 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and let a licensed agent shop multiple carriers at once.

faqs.q: What is final expense insurance?
faqs.a: Final expense insurance is a small whole life policy, usually $5,000 to $40,000, designed to cover funeral, burial, cremation, and final medical bills. It requires no medical exam in most cases, builds cash value, and pays a tax-free lump sum to your beneficiary. Premiums stay level and the policy never expires while paid.

faqs.q: How much does final expense insurance cost per month?
faqs.a: Costs depend on age, health, tobacco use, and benefit amount. A healthy non-smoker in their 60s often pays roughly $30 to $70 monthly for $10,000 in coverage, with rates rising at older ages. Guaranteed issue plans cost more. For an exact quote, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

faqs.q: Do I need a medical exam for final expense insurance?
faqs.a: Usually not. Most final expense policies use simplified issue underwriting with a short health questionnaire, no physical exam. Guaranteed issue plans ask no health questions at all. No-exam coverage means many seniors can be approved within days rather than weeks.

faqs.q: What does final expense insurance cover?
faqs.a: It pays a tax-free cash benefit to your beneficiary, who can use it for any final costs: funeral, burial or cremation, headstone, outstanding medical bills, credit card balances, or other debts. The money is not restricted to funeral services, giving your family full flexibility.

faqs.q: Is final expense insurance the same as burial insurance?
faqs.a: Yes. Final expense, burial, and funeral insurance are different marketing names for the same product — a small whole life policy that pays cash to a beneficiary. Unlike pre-need plans bought through a funeral home, the cash benefit can be used for any provider or expense.

faqs.q: Can seniors with health problems get final expense insurance?
faqs.a: Yes. Guaranteed issue final expense plans ask no health questions and accept nearly all applicants, though they typically include a two- to three-year graded death benefit period. Seniors with diabetes, heart disease, or COPD often qualify for simplified issue plans with better rates. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to find your fit.

faqs.q: How much coverage do I need for final expenses?
faqs.a: Most seniors choose $10,000 to $20,000. The 2023 median U.S. funeral with burial cost about $8,300 (NFDA), and cremation costs less. Add any debts or medical bills you want cleared. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP can help you size coverage to your family's needs.

faqs.q: Does final expense insurance expire?
faqs.a: No. Final expense insurance is permanent whole life coverage. As long as you pay the level premiums, it never expires and the death benefit stays in place for life, unlike term insurance that ends after a set number of years.

page.intro: Final expense insurance gives seniors an affordable way to leave cash, not debt, behind. Here is what it covers and how to get it.

sections.h2: How does final expense insurance work?
sections.body: Final expense insurance is permanent whole life coverage with a smaller benefit than standard life policies. You pick a death benefit, usually $5,000 to $40,000, and pay a level premium that never increases. The policy builds modest cash value and stays in force for life as long as premiums are paid. When you pass, your named beneficiary receives a tax-free lump sum they can spend on funeral costs, burial or cremation, medical bills, or any other need. Most plans use simplified or guaranteed issue underwriting, so no medical exam is required and approval is often quick.

sections.h2: Why seniors choose final expense coverage
sections.body: Final expense insurance is popular because it is easy to qualify for and the cash is unrestricted. Families can use it for whatever matters most, instead of being locked into a single funeral home like pre-need plans. Benefits are paid quickly, often within days of a claim, so loved ones are not stuck fronting thousands of dollars. With the 2023 median funeral plus burial near $8,300 (NFDA), even a modest policy can shield a family from a sudden financial burden during an already hard time.

faqs.q: What is final expense insurance in simple terms?
faqs.a: It is a small whole life insurance policy that pays cash to your family to cover your funeral, burial, and final bills. Benefits typically range from $5,000 to $40,000, most plans need no medical exam, and the coverage never expires while premiums are paid.

faqs.q: How much does final expense insurance cost?
faqs.a: Premiums vary by age, health, tobacco use, and benefit amount. A non-smoker in their 60s often pays roughly $30 to $70 monthly for $10,000 of coverage. For an exact, personalized quote, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

faqs.q: Is final expense insurance worth it?
faqs.a: For seniors without enough savings set aside for funeral costs, it usually is. It converts small monthly premiums into a tax-free lump sum that spares your family from paying thousands out of pocket. The benefit can also cover lingering medical bills or debts.

faqs.q: Can I be denied final expense insurance?
faqs.a: Simplified issue plans can decline applicants with serious recent health events, but guaranteed issue plans accept nearly everyone with no health questions. That means almost every senior can find some form of coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to match your health to the right plan.

faqs.q: How fast can I get covered?
faqs.a: Many no-exam final expense policies approve applicants within a few days. Simplified issue plans answer a short health questionnaire, while guaranteed issue plans skip questions entirely, speeding approval for seniors who need coverage quickly.

Most plans need no medical exam. It is the same product as final expense or funeral insurance, just a different name. The 2023 median U.S. burial funeral cost about $8,300 (NFDA).
page.intro: Burial insurance helps seniors cover the rising cost of a funeral and burial without leaving the bill to family. Here is how it works.

sections.h2: What does burial insurance cover?
sections.body: Burial insurance pays a cash benefit your family can use for the casket, plot, headstone, funeral service, transportation, and related costs. Because the benefit is paid directly to your beneficiary as cash, it is not limited to burial alone — leftover funds can clear medical bills or other debts. The policy is whole life, so it never expires while premiums are paid, and it builds modest cash value over time. Most seniors qualify without a medical exam, answering only a few health questions or, with guaranteed issue, none at all.

sections.h2: How much burial insurance do you need?
sections.body: Coverage should reflect what a funeral and burial cost in your area. The 2023 national median for a funeral with viewing and burial was about $8,300 (National Funeral Directors Association), not including the cemetery plot, which can add thousands more. Most seniors choose $10,000 to $15,000 to cover services with a cushion for extras. If you also want to leave money for medical bills or small debts, size up accordingly. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) can help you choose the right amount.

faqs.q: What is burial insurance?
faqs.a: Burial insurance is a small whole life policy that pays your family cash to cover funeral and burial costs. Benefits usually range from $5,000 to $25,000, most plans require no medical exam, and coverage never expires while premiums are paid. It is the same as final expense insurance.

faqs.q: How much does burial insurance cost?
faqs.a: Premiums depend on age, health, tobacco use, and benefit size. Many seniors pay between $30 and $80 a month for $10,000 of coverage. Guaranteed issue plans cost more. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for a personalized quote.

faqs.q: Do you need a medical exam for burial insurance?
faqs.a: Usually no. Most burial insurance uses simplified issue underwriting with a short health questionnaire and no physical exam. Guaranteed issue plans ask no health questions at all, so nearly any senior can qualify.

faqs.q: Is burial insurance the same as life insurance?
faqs.a: Burial insurance is a type of whole life insurance with a smaller benefit aimed at covering funeral costs. It works like other whole life policies — permanent coverage, level premiums, cash value — but with simpler underwriting and lower face amounts suited to seniors.

faqs.q: Can my family use burial insurance for other expenses?
faqs.a: Yes. The benefit is paid as cash to your beneficiary, who can use it for anything — funeral, burial, cremation, medical bills, or debts. It is not restricted to burial services, unlike pre-need plans bought through a funeral home.

Most plans require no medical exam and accept applicants into their 80s. It is the same product as burial or final expense insurance. The 2023 median U.S. funeral cost about $8,300 (NFDA).
page.intro: Funeral insurance lets seniors plan ahead so loved ones are not left with the bill. Here is how seniors can qualify and what it costs.

sections.h2: How does funeral insurance for seniors work?
sections.body: Funeral insurance is whole life coverage tailored for seniors who want to pre-fund their final arrangements. You select a benefit amount, pay a level premium, and your beneficiary receives a tax-free cash payout when you pass. The cash can cover the service, casket or urn, plot, headstone, and related costs. Because face amounts are modest and underwriting is simplified, seniors well into their 70s and 80s can often qualify. No-exam approval means you answer a few health questions, or none at all with guaranteed issue, and coverage can begin within days.

sections.h2: Why seniors buy funeral insurance
sections.body: The cost of a funeral has climbed steadily, with the 2023 median for a funeral and burial near $8,300 (NFDA). Many seniors do not want that expense to fall on children or a surviving spouse. Funeral insurance turns a manageable monthly premium into a guaranteed cash benefit that arrives quickly when it is needed most. Unlike pre-need plans tied to one funeral home, this cash gives the family freedom to choose any provider and to redirect leftover funds toward other final expenses or debts.

faqs.q: What is funeral insurance for seniors?
faqs.a: Funeral insurance for seniors is a small whole life policy that pays cash to cover funeral, burial, or cremation costs. Benefits typically run $5,000 to $25,000, most plans need no medical exam, and the coverage never expires while premiums are paid. It is the same as burial or final expense insurance.

faqs.q: What is the maximum age to buy funeral insurance?
faqs.a: Many carriers accept applicants up to age 85, and some guaranteed issue plans go to 89. Rates rise with age, so applying sooner locks in lower premiums. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to find carriers that accept your age.

faqs.q: How much does funeral insurance cost for seniors?
faqs.a: Premiums depend on age, health, and benefit amount. A senior in their 70s might pay $50 to $120 monthly for $10,000 of coverage. Guaranteed issue plans cost more. A licensed agent can give you an exact quote at 1-800-MEDIGAP.

faqs.q: Can seniors with health issues get funeral insurance?
faqs.a: Yes. Guaranteed issue funeral plans ask no health questions and accept nearly all seniors, usually with a two- to three-year graded benefit period. Those in better health can qualify for simplified issue plans with immediate coverage and lower rates.

faqs.q: Does funeral insurance pay out quickly?
faqs.a: Yes. Once a valid claim and death certificate are submitted, benefits are typically paid within days to a couple of weeks, so families are not forced to pay funeral costs out of pocket while waiting.

page.h1: Final Expense Insurance for Seniors Over 70
page.heroSubhead: No-exam whole life coverage designed for seniors in their 70s and beyond.
page.quickAnswer: Seniors over 70 can readily buy final expense insurance, small whole life policies of roughly $5,000 to $25,000 with no medical exam. Most carriers accept applicants through age 85, and guaranteed issue plans accept nearly everyone. Premiums rise with age, so applying sooner locks in lower rates. The 2023 median U.S. funeral cost about $8,300 (NFDA).
page.intro: Turning 70 does not shut you out of coverage. Final expense insurance is built for older seniors. Here is how to qualify and what to expect.

sections.h2: Can you get final expense insurance after age 70?
sections.body: Yes. Final expense insurance is specifically designed for older adults, and most carriers accept applicants well into their 80s. Two paths exist. Simplified issue plans ask a short list of health questions and reward good health with lower premiums and immediate coverage. Guaranteed issue plans skip health questions entirely and accept nearly every applicant, with a graded benefit period of two to three years. For a healthy 70-something, simplified issue usually offers the best value; for those with serious conditions, guaranteed issue ensures coverage no one can decline.

sections.h2: What seniors over 70 should expect to pay
sections.body: Premiums climb with age because life expectancy is shorter, so a policy that costs $40 a month at 60 may cost more at 72. As a rough guide, a non-smoking 72-year-old might pay $70 to $140 a month for $10,000 in coverage, depending on health and carrier. The longer you wait, the higher the rate, which is why applying now matters. Because pricing varies sharply between carriers, comparing several is the only way to find the lowest rate. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will shop carriers for you.

faqs.q: Can a 70-year-old get final expense insurance?
faqs.a: Yes, easily. Final expense insurance is designed for older seniors, and most carriers accept applicants through age 85. Healthy seniors qualify for simplified issue plans with immediate coverage, while guaranteed issue plans accept nearly everyone regardless of health.

faqs.q: How much does final expense insurance cost at age 70?
faqs.a: A non-smoking 70- to 72-year-old might pay roughly $70 to $140 a month for $10,000 in coverage, varying by health and carrier. Rates rise each year you wait. For an exact quote, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

faqs.q: Do seniors over 70 need a medical exam?
faqs.a: No. Final expense plans for seniors over 70 use no-exam underwriting. Simplified issue plans ask a few health questions; guaranteed issue plans ask none. Either way, there is no physical exam or bloodwork required.

faqs.q: Is it too late to buy coverage at 75 or older?
faqs.a: No. Many carriers accept applicants up to 85, and some guaranteed issue plans go to 89. While premiums are higher at older ages, coverage remains available. Applying as soon as possible keeps your rate lower.

faqs.q: What if I have diabetes or heart disease?
faqs.a: You can still qualify. Many simplified issue carriers accept controlled diabetes or stable heart conditions. If health questions are a barrier, guaranteed issue plans accept you with no questions asked. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP to match your health to the right carrier.

page.metaDescription: Is burial insurance worth it? See when it pays off and when to skip it. Compare senior plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: An honest look at when burial insurance makes sense for seniors.
page.quickAnswer: Burial insurance is worth it for seniors who lack savings set aside for funeral costs and want to spare family the bill. It converts small monthly premiums into a tax-free cash benefit. It may not be worth it if you have ample savings or can self-fund. The 2023 median U.S. funeral cost about $8,300 (NFDA).
page.intro: Burial insurance is not right for everyone. Here is a straight answer on when it pays off and when you can skip it.

sections.h2: When burial insurance is worth it
sections.body: Burial insurance makes the most sense if you do not have several thousand dollars set aside specifically for final expenses, or if you do not want to leave that burden to your spouse or children. It is also valuable if your health makes traditional life insurance expensive or unavailable, since most burial plans require no medical exam. For seniors on fixed incomes, the trade is simple: a modest monthly premium today guarantees a tax-free lump sum that covers the roughly $8,300 median funeral cost (NFDA) when the time comes, so loved ones are not scrambling to pay.

sections.h2: When you might skip burial insurance
sections.body: If you already have enough liquid savings earmarked for funeral costs, or a fully funded life insurance policy that covers final expenses, a separate burial policy may be unnecessary. Over many years, premiums can add up to more than the death benefit, especially on guaranteed issue plans bought at older ages. A pre-paid funeral or a payable-on-death bank account are alternatives some seniors prefer. The right choice depends on your savings, health, and how much certainty you want. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) can run the numbers honestly with you.

faqs.q: Is burial insurance a good idea?
faqs.a: For seniors without dedicated savings for funeral costs, yes. It guarantees a tax-free cash benefit so family is not stuck paying thousands. For those with ample savings or existing life insurance covering final costs, it may be unnecessary. The decision depends on your finances and health.

faqs.q: Does burial insurance cost more than it pays out?
faqs.a: It can, if you buy a guaranteed issue plan at an advanced age and live many years. For most seniors, though, the policy pays out far more than premiums paid, especially if death occurs in the earlier years of coverage. Comparing plans helps you find the best value.

faqs.q: What are alternatives to burial insurance?
faqs.a: Alternatives include a dedicated savings account, a payable-on-death bank account, a pre-paid funeral plan through a funeral home, or using an existing life insurance policy. Each has trade-offs in flexibility and cost. A licensed agent can help you compare.

faqs.q: Is burial insurance worth it for someone in poor health?
faqs.a: Often yes. If health problems make standard life insurance costly or unavailable, guaranteed issue burial insurance offers coverage with no health questions. The peace of mind and family protection frequently outweigh the higher premiums these plans carry.

faqs.q: How do I decide if burial insurance is right for me?
faqs.a: Add up your final-expense savings, weigh your health and life insurance, and consider whether family could afford the roughly $8,300 median funeral cost. If there is a gap, burial insurance fills it. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for a no-pressure review.

page.metaDescription: Guaranteed issue final expense insurance with no health questions and no exam. Compare plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: Final expense coverage with no health questions and no exam.
page.quickAnswer: Guaranteed issue final expense insurance accepts nearly every applicant with no health questions and no medical exam. In exchange, plans include a graded death benefit period, typically two to three years, during which non-accidental death refunds premiums plus interest. Benefits usually range from $5,000 to $25,000. It is ideal for seniors declined elsewhere.
page.intro: If health problems have made coverage hard to get, guaranteed issue final expense insurance is built for you. Here is how it works.

sections.h2: How guaranteed issue final expense works
sections.body: Guaranteed issue plans are whole life policies that ask no health questions and require no medical exam, so approval is essentially automatic within the carrier's age limits, usually up to 80 or 85. The trade-off is a graded death benefit. If you die of natural causes during the first two to three years, the policy refunds your premiums plus interest rather than the full benefit; accidental death is typically covered in full from day one. After the graded period, the full benefit applies. This structure lets carriers accept everyone while protecting against immediate claims.

sections.h2: Who should choose guaranteed issue?
sections.body: Guaranteed issue is the right fit for seniors who have been declined for other coverage, or who have serious conditions like recent cancer, advanced heart disease, or oxygen-dependent COPD. If you can answer health questions favorably, a simplified issue plan usually costs less and pays the full benefit immediately, so guaranteed issue is best reserved for those who cannot qualify otherwise. Because rates and graded terms differ by carrier, comparing options matters. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will confirm whether you can qualify for a better simplified issue plan first.

faqs.q: What is guaranteed issue final expense insurance?
faqs.a: It is a whole life policy that accepts nearly all applicants with no health questions and no medical exam. Benefits typically range from $5,000 to $25,000. Plans include a two- to three-year graded period during which natural-cause death refunds premiums plus interest instead of the full benefit.

faqs.q: What is the graded death benefit period?
faqs.a: The graded period is the first two to three years of a guaranteed issue policy. If you die of natural causes during this time, the policy returns your paid premiums plus interest rather than the full benefit. Accidental death is usually covered fully from day one, and the full benefit applies afterward.

faqs.q: Who should buy guaranteed issue coverage?
faqs.a: Seniors who have been declined elsewhere or who have serious health conditions like recent cancer or oxygen-dependent COPD. If you can pass health questions, a simplified issue plan is cheaper and pays immediately, so guaranteed issue is best for those who cannot qualify any other way.

faqs.q: Can anyone be denied guaranteed issue insurance?
faqs.a: Within the carrier's age limits, almost no one is denied because there are no health questions. The main restrictions are age (often up to 80 or 85) and state availability. That is what makes it a reliable option for hard-to-insure seniors.

faqs.q: How much does guaranteed issue final expense cost?
faqs.a: It costs more than simplified issue because the carrier takes on more risk. Premiums vary by age and benefit amount. To compare guaranteed issue rates across carriers, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for a personalized quote.

page.metaTitle: How Much Does Burial Insurance Cost? | 1-800-MEDIGAP
page.metaDescription: How much does burial insurance cost? See what affects your rate and how to compare. Free quotes. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.h1: How Much Does Burial Insurance Cost?
page.heroSubhead: What drives your premium and how to find the lowest rate.
page.quickAnswer: Burial insurance typically costs $30 to $100+ a month, depending on your age, health, gender, tobacco use, and benefit amount. A healthy non-smoker in their 60s often pays $30 to $70 monthly for $10,000 in coverage; rates rise with age. Guaranteed issue plans cost more. Compare carriers to find the lowest rate.
page.intro: Burial insurance pricing depends on a handful of factors. Here is what drives your rate and how to keep it low.

sections.h2: What affects your burial insurance premium?
sections.body: Five main factors set your rate: age (older means higher), gender (women generally pay less due to longer life expectancy), health and any conditions, tobacco use (smokers pay notably more), and the death benefit you choose. Plan type matters too — simplified issue plans for healthier applicants cost less than guaranteed issue plans that skip health questions. Because each carrier weighs these factors differently, the same coverage can vary by hundreds of dollars a year between companies. That is why comparing multiple A-rated carriers is the single best way to lower your premium.

sections.h2: Sample monthly costs by profile
sections.body: As a general guide for $10,000 in coverage: a healthy non-smoking woman in her early 60s might pay around $30 to $45 a month, while a man the same age pays a bit more. By the early 70s, premiums often range from $60 to $120, and guaranteed issue plans at any age cost more than simplified issue. Smokers can pay 40% or more above non-smoker rates. These are illustrative ranges, not quotes — your actual price depends on your details. For real numbers, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and an agent will compare carriers.

faqs.q: How much does burial insurance cost per month?
faqs.a: Most seniors pay $30 to $100 or more monthly, depending on age, health, gender, tobacco use, and benefit amount. A healthy non-smoker in their 60s often pays $30 to $70 for $10,000 of coverage. For an exact quote, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

faqs.q: What makes burial insurance more expensive?
faqs.a: Older age, tobacco use, poor health, a larger death benefit, and choosing a guaranteed issue plan all raise premiums. Men generally pay more than women. Comparing carriers helps offset these factors because each prices risk differently.

faqs.q: Is burial insurance cheaper if I'm healthy?
faqs.a: Yes. Healthy seniors qualify for simplified issue plans with lower premiums and immediate full coverage. If you can answer health questions favorably, you will almost always pay less than with a guaranteed issue plan.

faqs.q: How can I lower my burial insurance cost?
faqs.a: Apply while you are younger and healthier, quit tobacco, choose only the coverage you need, and compare several carriers. Because identical coverage can differ by hundreds of dollars a year between companies, shopping around is the most effective way to save.

faqs.q: Do burial insurance premiums increase over time?
faqs.a: No. Burial insurance is whole life coverage with level premiums that never increase. The rate you lock in at purchase stays the same for life, as long as you keep paying.

page.metaDescription: Cremation insurance for seniors pays cash for cremation and final costs, no exam. Compare plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: Affordable coverage that pays cremation and final costs in cash.
page.quickAnswer: Cremation insurance for seniors is a small whole life policy that pays a cash benefit toward cremation and related final costs. Because cremation costs less than burial, smaller benefits of $2,000 to $10,000 often suffice. Most plans need no medical exam. A direct cremation typically runs far below the $8,300 median burial funeral (NFDA).
page.intro: Choosing cremation can lower final costs, and cremation insurance makes sure the bill is covered. Here is how it works for seniors.

sections.h2: How cremation insurance works for seniors
sections.body: Cremation insurance is the same product as final expense or burial insurance — a small whole life policy paid in cash to your beneficiary — sized for the lower cost of cremation. Because cremation, especially direct cremation, costs much less than a traditional burial, many seniors choose smaller benefit amounts of $2,000 to $10,000. The cash can cover the cremation service, urn, memorial, and any remaining medical bills. Most plans require no medical exam, using simplified or guaranteed issue underwriting, so seniors in their 70s and 80s can typically qualify and lock in level premiums for life.

sections.h2: Why cremation insurance can cost less
sections.body: Cremation is generally far cheaper than burial. A traditional funeral with viewing and burial ran a median of about $8,300 in 2023, while a funeral with cremation was notably lower, and direct cremation lower still (NFDA). Because the funeral expense is smaller, you may need less coverage, which keeps premiums down. That said, you can size the benefit higher if you also want to leave money for a memorial service or to clear debts. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) can right-size a policy to your plans.

faqs.q: What is cremation insurance for seniors?
faqs.a: It is a small whole life policy that pays cash toward cremation and related final costs. It is the same as final expense or burial insurance, just sized for cremation's lower cost. Benefits often range from $2,000 to $10,000, with no medical exam required for most plans.

faqs.q: How much cremation insurance do I need?
faqs.a: It depends on whether you want a simple direct cremation or a service with a memorial. Direct cremation costs far less than a traditional burial, so many seniors choose $2,000 to $10,000. Add more if you want funds for a memorial or debts. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP for help.

faqs.q: Is cremation insurance cheaper than burial insurance?
faqs.a: It can be, because cremation costs less than burial, so you often need a smaller benefit, which means lower premiums. The policy type is the same; the cost difference comes from choosing a smaller death benefit suited to cremation expenses.

faqs.q: Does cremation insurance require a medical exam?
faqs.a: Usually no. Most cremation insurance uses simplified issue underwriting with a short health questionnaire, or guaranteed issue with no health questions at all. Neither requires a physical exam, so approval is typically fast.

faqs.q: Can I use cremation insurance for other expenses?
faqs.a: Yes. The benefit is paid as cash to your beneficiary, who can use it for the cremation, an urn, a memorial service, or any remaining medical bills and debts. It is not restricted to cremation services alone.

page.metaDescription: How to find the best final expense insurance companies by rating, rates, and approval. Compare carriers free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: How to compare carriers on strength, price, and approval terms.
page.quickAnswer: The best final expense insurance companies combine strong financial ratings (A.M. Best A or higher), competitive rates for your age and health, fast no-exam approval, and immediate full coverage when possible. No single carrier is best for everyone — the right company depends on your health profile, so comparing several is essential.
page.intro: There is no one best final expense company for every senior. Here is how to judge carriers and find your best match.

sections.h2: What makes a final expense company the best for you
sections.body: The strongest final expense carriers share a few traits: a high financial strength rating (A.M. Best A or above) so they can reliably pay claims, competitive pricing for your specific age and health, simplified underwriting that grants immediate full coverage rather than a long graded period, and a smooth, fast approval process. Just as important is fit: a carrier that is best-priced for a healthy 65-year-old non-smoker may be expensive for a 75-year-old with diabetes. Because each company underwrites conditions differently, the best company is the one that rates your particular health most favorably.

sections.h2: How to compare carriers the smart way
sections.body: Rather than chasing brand names, compare carriers on the metrics that matter: financial rating, monthly premium for your exact coverage, whether coverage is immediate or graded, and the company's reputation for paying claims promptly. Pricing for identical coverage can differ by hundreds of dollars a year, so getting multiple quotes is the only way to know who is best for you. An independent licensed agent can shop many A-rated carriers at once and flag which one treats your health history most kindly. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to compare top companies in one call.

faqs.q: Which is the best final expense insurance company?
faqs.a: There is no single best company for everyone. The best carrier depends on your age, health, and budget, because each insurer prices conditions differently. The smart approach is comparing several A-rated carriers to see which one offers the best rate and terms for your situation.

faqs.q: What rating should a final expense company have?
faqs.a: Look for an A.M. Best financial strength rating of A (Excellent) or higher. A strong rating signals the company has the financial reserves to reliably pay claims, which is the most important thing a final expense policy must do.

faqs.q: How do I compare final expense companies?
faqs.a: Compare financial ratings, the monthly premium for identical coverage, whether the benefit is immediate or graded, and claim-paying reputation. Because prices vary widely, get quotes from several carriers. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP can do this for you in one call.

faqs.q: Are big-name insurers always the best choice?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. A well-known brand may not offer the lowest rate or the most favorable underwriting for your health. Smaller A-rated carriers often beat big names on price for specific health profiles, which is why comparing several matters more than brand recognition.

faqs.q: How can I find the best company for my health?
faqs.a: An independent licensed agent can match your health history to the carrier most likely to approve you at the best rate and with immediate coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to have several top carriers compared for your situation.

page.metaDescription: How to pick the best burial insurance companies by rating, price, and approval. Compare carriers free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

page.quickAnswer: The best burial insurance companies pair strong financial ratings (A.M. Best A or higher) with competitive rates, no-exam approval, and immediate full coverage when your health allows. The best company varies by applicant because each insurer prices health conditions differently, so comparing several A-rated carriers is the key to the lowest rate.
page.intro: The best burial insurance company is the one that fits your health and budget. Here is how to compare and choose wisely.

sections.h2: What separates the best burial insurers
sections.body: Top burial insurance companies share strong financial strength ratings, transparent pricing, and underwriting that rewards your health with immediate coverage instead of a multi-year graded period. They also have a track record of paying claims quickly so families are not left waiting. But best is personal: an insurer with great rates for a healthy applicant may rate a senior with COPD or recent heart trouble much higher. Because each carrier treats conditions differently, the best company for you is the one that underwrites your specific health history most favorably and still carries an A.M. Best rating of A or above.

sections.h2: Comparing burial insurance companies the right way
sections.body: Skip the temptation to pick by brand name alone. Instead, line up carriers on the factors that matter: financial rating, monthly premium for the exact same benefit, immediate versus graded coverage, and claim-paying reputation. Identical coverage can vary by hundreds of dollars a year between companies, so quotes from several insurers reveal who is truly best for you. An independent licensed agent can shop many A-rated burial carriers at once and identify which one offers you the best price and terms. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to compare in a single call.

faqs.q: Which company has the best burial insurance?
faqs.a: No single company is best for everyone. Because each insurer prices age and health differently, the best burial company depends on your profile. Comparing several A-rated carriers is the only reliable way to find the one offering you the lowest rate and best terms.

faqs.q: What financial rating should a burial insurer have?
faqs.a: Choose insurers with an A.M. Best rating of A (Excellent) or higher. A strong rating means the company has the reserves to pay claims dependably, which is the core promise of any burial policy.

faqs.q: How do I compare burial insurance companies?
faqs.a: Compare financial ratings, the premium for identical coverage, whether the benefit is immediate or graded, and how fast each pays claims. Get quotes from several carriers, since prices vary widely. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP can compare them for you.

faqs.q: Are smaller burial insurers safe to use?
faqs.a: Yes, as long as they carry a strong A.M. Best rating. Many smaller, highly rated carriers offer better prices or more favorable underwriting for specific health conditions than big-name brands, making them excellent choices for the right applicant.

faqs.q: How do I find the best burial insurer for my health?
faqs.a: Work with an independent licensed agent who can match your health history to the carrier most likely to approve you at the best rate with immediate coverage. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to compare top burial companies.

page.metaDescription: Guaranteed acceptance burial insurance with no health questions and no exam. Compare plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: Burial coverage with no health questions and no exam.
page.quickAnswer: Guaranteed acceptance burial insurance accepts nearly every applicant within age limits with no health questions and no medical exam. In return, plans use a graded benefit period of two to three years, during which natural-cause death refunds premiums plus interest. Benefits usually range from $5,000 to $25,000. It is ideal for seniors with serious health conditions.
page.intro: If health issues have blocked you from coverage, guaranteed acceptance burial insurance is designed to say yes. Here is how it works.

sections.h2: How guaranteed acceptance burial insurance works
sections.body: Guaranteed acceptance burial insurance, also called guaranteed issue, is whole life coverage that asks no health questions and requires no exam. Approval is essentially automatic within the carrier's age range, typically up to 80 or 85. The trade-off is a graded death benefit: if you die of natural causes during the first two to three years, the policy refunds your premiums plus interest instead of the full benefit, while accidental death is usually covered in full immediately. After the graded period ends, the full benefit applies. This design lets insurers accept everyone while guarding against immediate large claims.

sections.h2: Is guaranteed acceptance right for you?
sections.body: Guaranteed acceptance is best for seniors who cannot qualify for other coverage because of serious health conditions, or who have been declined before. If you are reasonably healthy and can answer health questions, a simplified issue plan typically costs less and pays the full benefit from day one, so it is worth checking your eligibility there first. Because premiums and graded terms vary by carrier, comparing matters. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will confirm whether you qualify for a lower-cost simplified issue plan before defaulting to guaranteed acceptance.

faqs.q: What is guaranteed acceptance burial insurance?
faqs.a: It is a whole life burial policy that accepts nearly all applicants with no health questions and no medical exam. Benefits typically range from $5,000 to $25,000. Plans include a two- to three-year graded period during which natural-cause death refunds premiums plus interest rather than the full benefit.

faqs.q: Is anyone turned down for guaranteed acceptance coverage?
faqs.a: Within the carrier's age limits, almost no one is turned down because there are no health questions. The main limits are age, usually up to 80 or 85, and state availability. This makes it a dependable option for seniors who cannot get other coverage.

faqs.q: What is the catch with guaranteed acceptance insurance?
faqs.a: The main trade-off is the graded death benefit: natural-cause death in the first two to three years refunds premiums plus interest instead of the full payout. Premiums are also higher than medically underwritten plans. After the graded period, the full benefit is paid.

faqs.q: Should I choose guaranteed acceptance or simplified issue?
faqs.a: If you can answer health questions favorably, simplified issue is usually cheaper and pays the full benefit immediately. Choose guaranteed acceptance only if health problems prevent you from qualifying elsewhere. A licensed agent can check both options for you.

faqs.q: How do I get guaranteed acceptance burial insurance?
faqs.a: Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427). A licensed agent will confirm your age eligibility, compare guaranteed acceptance carriers, and first check whether you qualify for a lower-cost simplified issue plan with immediate coverage.

page.metaDescription: Burial insurance for seniors over 75 with no exam and easy approval. Compare rates free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: No-exam burial coverage built for seniors 75 and older.
page.quickAnswer: Seniors over 75 can still buy burial insurance. Most carriers accept applicants through age 85, and guaranteed acceptance plans accept nearly everyone with no health questions. Benefits typically run $5,000 to $25,000 with no medical exam. Premiums are higher at this age, so applying promptly locks in the best available rate.
page.intro: Being over 75 does not close the door on coverage. Burial insurance is made for older seniors. Here is how to qualify.

sections.h2: Can you buy burial insurance after 75?
sections.body: Yes. Burial insurance is specifically designed for older seniors, and most carriers accept applicants up to 85, with some guaranteed acceptance plans going to 89. Two routes exist. Simplified issue plans ask a short health questionnaire and reward good health with lower premiums and immediate coverage. Guaranteed acceptance plans ask no health questions and accept nearly everyone, with a two- to three-year graded benefit period. For a healthy 75-year-old, simplified issue often offers better value; for those with serious conditions, guaranteed acceptance ensures coverage no insurer can decline.

sections.h2: What to expect on price and coverage at 75+
sections.body: Premiums are higher at 75 and above because life expectancy is shorter, so benefit amounts are often kept modest, commonly $5,000 to $15,000, to keep payments affordable. A non-smoking 76-year-old might pay roughly $90 to $170 a month for $10,000 in coverage, varying by health and carrier. Rates climb every year you wait, making prompt application important. Because carriers price older applicants very differently, comparing several is the best way to find an affordable rate. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will shop carriers for you.

faqs.q: Can a 75-year-old get burial insurance?
faqs.a: Yes. Most carriers accept applicants through age 85, and some guaranteed acceptance plans go to 89. Healthy seniors can qualify for simplified issue plans with immediate coverage, while guaranteed acceptance plans accept nearly everyone with no health questions.

faqs.q: How much does burial insurance cost at 75?
faqs.a: A non-smoking 75- to 76-year-old might pay roughly $90 to $170 a month for $10,000 in coverage, depending on health and carrier. Rates rise each year. For an exact quote, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).

faqs.q: Do seniors over 75 need a medical exam?
faqs.a: No. Burial insurance for seniors over 75 uses no-exam underwriting. Simplified issue plans ask a few health questions; guaranteed acceptance plans ask none. There is no physical exam or bloodwork at any age.

faqs.q: What if I have serious health problems at 75?
faqs.a: You can still get covered. Guaranteed acceptance plans accept nearly all applicants with no health questions, typically with a graded benefit period. Seniors with manageable conditions may qualify for simplified issue plans offering immediate coverage and lower rates.

faqs.q: Is it worth buying burial insurance at 75?
faqs.a: For seniors without savings earmarked for funeral costs, yes. It guarantees a tax-free cash benefit so family is not left paying thousands. If you have ample savings or existing life insurance covering final costs, it may be less necessary. A licensed agent can help you decide.

page.metaDescription: No medical exam burial insurance with fast approval for seniors. Compare plans free. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427).
page.heroSubhead: Burial coverage with no physical, no bloodwork, fast approval.
page.quickAnswer: No medical exam burial insurance approves seniors without a physical, bloodwork, or lab tests. Simplified issue plans ask a short health questionnaire and offer lower rates with immediate coverage; guaranteed issue plans ask no health questions at all. Benefits typically range from $5,000 to $25,000, and approval often takes just days.
page.intro: No-exam burial insurance skips the physical so seniors can get covered fast. Here is how the two no-exam options work.

sections.h2: How no medical exam burial insurance works
sections.body: No-exam burial insurance replaces the traditional physical and bloodwork with either a short health questionnaire or no questions at all. With simplified issue, you answer a handful of yes/no health questions; if you pass, you get lower rates and full coverage from day one. With guaranteed issue, there are no health questions, so nearly everyone is accepted, but a two- to three-year graded period applies. Both skip labs entirely. Carriers verify some information through prescription and database checks rather than an exam, which is why approval can happen in days instead of weeks.

sections.h2: Which no-exam option is right for you?
sections.body: If you are in reasonable health and can answer the questions favorably, simplified issue is the better deal: lower premiums and immediate coverage. If you have serious conditions or have been declined before, guaranteed issue guarantees acceptance despite the graded period and higher cost. Many seniors assume they need guaranteed issue when they actually qualify for cheaper simplified issue, so it pays to check both. A licensed agent at 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) can run your health questions across carriers and find the best no-exam plan you qualify for.

faqs.q: Is there really burial insurance with no medical exam?
faqs.a: Yes. Most burial insurance requires no medical exam. Simplified issue plans ask a short health questionnaire, and guaranteed issue plans ask no health questions at all. Neither requires a physical, bloodwork, or lab tests, and approval often takes just days.

faqs.q: How do insurers approve me without an exam?
faqs.a: Instead of a physical, carriers verify information through prescription history and database checks, plus your answers to any health questions. This lets them assess risk quickly, which is why no-exam approval can happen within days rather than the weeks a traditional exam requires.

faqs.q: Is no-exam burial insurance more expensive?
faqs.a: Simplified issue no-exam plans are competitively priced for healthy applicants. Guaranteed issue no-exam plans cost more because they accept everyone without health questions. The cost depends on which no-exam type you qualify for, not the absence of an exam itself.

faqs.q: How fast can I get no-exam burial insurance?
faqs.a: Often within a few days. Because there is no physical to schedule and no lab results to wait on, carriers can review your application and database checks quickly, making no-exam plans one of the fastest ways for seniors to get covered.

faqs.q: Which no-exam plan should I choose?
faqs.a: If you can answer health questions favorably, simplified issue offers lower rates and immediate coverage. If health problems are a barrier, guaranteed issue accepts you with no questions. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to find the best no-exam plan you qualify for.

page.quickAnswer: Burial insurance with no health questions is guaranteed issue coverage that accepts nearly every applicant within age limits, no exam and no medical questionnaire. In exchange, plans include a two- to three-year graded period during which natural-cause death refunds premiums plus interest. Benefits typically range from $5,000 to $25,000.
page.intro: Some burial plans ask zero health questions and accept nearly everyone. Here is how no-health-question coverage works.

sections.h2: How no-health-question burial insurance works
sections.body: Burial insurance with no health questions is the same as guaranteed issue or guaranteed acceptance coverage. The application skips all medical questions and any exam, so approval is essentially automatic within the carrier's age limits, usually up to 80 or 85. To balance accepting everyone, these plans use a graded death benefit: natural-cause death in the first two to three years refunds your premiums plus interest rather than the full benefit, while accidental death is typically covered in full from day one. After the graded period, the full benefit applies for any cause.

sections.h2: When to choose a no-health-question plan
sections.body: No-health-question coverage is built for seniors with serious conditions or those who have been declined elsewhere, because acceptance is guaranteed regardless of health. If you can answer a few simple health questions favorably, however, a simplified issue plan usually costs less and pays the full benefit immediately, so it is worth checking first. Do not assume you need a no-question plan until an agent reviews your eligibility. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will see whether you qualify for a lower-cost option before recommending a no-health-question plan.

faqs.q: Is there burial insurance with no health questions?
faqs.a: Yes. Guaranteed issue burial insurance asks no health questions and requires no exam, accepting nearly all applicants within age limits. In exchange, it uses a two- to three-year graded period during which natural-cause death refunds premiums plus interest instead of the full benefit.

faqs.q: Can I be denied a no-health-question plan?
faqs.a: Within the carrier's age limits, almost no one is denied because there are no health questions to fail. The main restrictions are age, usually up to 80 or 85, and state availability, making these plans reliable for hard-to-insure seniors.

faqs.q: Why does no-question coverage have a waiting period?
faqs.a: Because insurers accept everyone without reviewing health, they use a two- to three-year graded period to protect against immediate claims. Natural-cause death during this time refunds premiums plus interest; accidental death is usually covered fully right away.

faqs.q: Is no-health-question burial insurance more expensive?
faqs.a: Yes, it typically costs more than plans that ask health questions, because the insurer accepts more risk by skipping underwriting. If your health allows you to answer questions favorably, a simplified issue plan is usually cheaper with immediate coverage.

faqs.q: Should I get a no-question plan or answer health questions?
faqs.a: If you can answer health questions favorably, a simplified issue plan is cheaper and pays immediately. Choose no-question coverage only if health problems prevent you from qualifying elsewhere. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to check both options.

page.quickAnswer: Burial insurance cost rises steadily with age because life expectancy shortens. For $10,000 in coverage, a non-smoker might pay $30 to $50 monthly in their 50s, $40 to $70 in their 60s, $70 to $140 in their 70s, and more beyond 80. Locking in coverage earlier secures a lower lifetime rate.
page.intro: Age is the biggest driver of burial insurance premiums. Here is roughly how cost climbs decade by decade.

sections.h2: How burial insurance cost rises with age
sections.body: Because burial insurance is whole life coverage, the insurer prices in your life expectancy at the time you apply. The older you are, the closer the expected payout, so premiums rise with each passing year. For $10,000 in coverage, a healthy non-smoker might see roughly $30 to $50 a month in their 50s, $40 to $70 in their 60s, $70 to $140 in their 70s, and higher still past 80. These are illustrative ranges, not quotes, and your gender, health, and tobacco use shift them. Once you lock in a rate, though, it stays level for life.

sections.h2: Why applying earlier saves money
sections.body: Every year you wait, your premium for the same coverage goes up, and a serious health diagnosis could push you from a low-cost simplified issue plan into a pricier guaranteed issue plan. Buying earlier locks in both a lower rate and easier underwriting, and because premiums are level, that rate never increases for life. Waiting rarely pays off. To see exactly what your age and health mean for your premium across multiple carriers, call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) and a licensed agent will compare real quotes for you.

faqs.q: How much does burial insurance cost by age?
faqs.a: For $10,000 in coverage, a non-smoker might pay roughly $30 to $50 monthly in their 50s, $40 to $70 in their 60s, $70 to $140 in their 70s, and more past 80. These are illustrative ranges; gender, health, and tobacco use affect the final price.

faqs.q: Why does burial insurance cost more as I age?
faqs.a: Burial insurance is whole life coverage priced on life expectancy. The older you are, the sooner the expected payout, so insurers charge higher premiums. This is why the same coverage costs progressively more each year you wait to apply.

faqs.q: Does my premium increase as I get older after I buy?
faqs.a: No. Once you purchase, burial insurance premiums are level and never increase for life. Age affects only the rate you lock in at purchase, which is why buying earlier secures a permanently lower premium.

faqs.q: Is burial insurance cheaper if I buy younger?
faqs.a: Yes. Premiums rise each year with age, and buying younger also means easier underwriting before health issues arise. Locking in coverage earlier secures a lower lifetime rate that never increases.

faqs.q: How do I get an exact price for my age?
faqs.a: Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427). A licensed agent will use your age, gender, health, and tobacco status to compare real quotes from multiple A-rated carriers and find your lowest available rate.

page.quickAnswer: To compare final expense insurance quotes, gather rates from several A-rated carriers for the same coverage amount, then weigh price, financial strength, and whether coverage is immediate or graded. Prices for identical coverage can differ by hundreds of dollars a year, so comparing multiple carriers is the single best way to lower your premium.
page.intro: Comparing final expense quotes is the surest way to avoid overpaying. Here is what to compare and how to do it fast.

sections.h2: What to compare across final expense quotes
sections.body: When you line up quotes, hold the coverage amount constant so you are comparing apples to apples, then look at four things: the monthly premium, the carrier's A.M. Best financial strength rating, whether coverage is immediate or uses a graded period, and the company's reputation for paying claims. The cheapest quote is not always best — a slightly higher premium with immediate full coverage can beat a low quote that carries a multi-year graded benefit. Comparing these details side by side reveals the true value, not just the headline price.

sections.h2: The fastest way to compare quotes
sections.body: You could request quotes one carrier at a time, but that is slow and exposes you to repeated sales calls. The faster path is an independent licensed agent who shops many A-rated final expense carriers at once and presents the best matches for your age and health in a single conversation. Because each carrier underwrites conditions differently, an agent can also steer you toward the company most likely to approve you at the lowest rate. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for a free, no-obligation comparison tailored to you.

faqs.q: How do I compare final expense insurance quotes?
faqs.a: Hold the coverage amount constant, then compare monthly premium, A.M. Best financial rating, immediate versus graded coverage, and claim-paying reputation across several carriers. Because identical coverage can vary by hundreds of dollars a year, comparing multiple carriers is the best way to save.

faqs.q: Why do final expense quotes vary so much?
faqs.a: Each carrier weighs age, gender, health, and tobacco use differently and sets its own underwriting rules, so the same coverage can be priced hundreds of dollars apart. That variation is exactly why comparing several quotes is worthwhile.

faqs.q: Is the cheapest final expense quote always best?
faqs.a: Not always. A low quote with a multi-year graded benefit period may be worse than a slightly higher one offering immediate full coverage. Always weigh price against coverage terms and the carrier's financial strength, not price alone.

faqs.q: How can I compare quotes without lots of sales calls?
faqs.a: Use an independent licensed agent who shops many carriers at once, so you get multiple quotes from a single conversation instead of fielding calls from each company. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) for a free comparison.

faqs.q: Is comparing final expense quotes free?
faqs.a: Yes. Comparing quotes through a licensed agent is free and carries no obligation. You see your options and rates across multiple A-rated carriers, then decide if and when to buy. Call 1-800-MEDIGAP (1-800-633-4427) to start.
